species.notes: Dromedary support is pinned to domestic-dromedary archive evidence. Comparator material from other camelids does not promote dromedary support automatically.

species_manifest.scientific_scope: Dromedary support is restricted to Camelus dromedarius evidence. Other camelid or broader comparator material does not promote dromedary support.
species_manifest.runtime_scope: Current runtime normalization and reporting remain human AADR-first until species-specific collectors and archive bridges are implemented.

coordinate_provenance_records.site_label: Site 1040 near Wadi Halfa dromedary context
coordinate_provenance_records.geocoder_or_gazetteer: named-place resolution to Wadi Halfa city anchor
coordinate_provenance_records.confidence_rationale: The archived article names Site 1040 near Wadi Halfa explicitly. The current coordinate is an approximate city-level geocode for Wadi Halfa rather than a published excavation coordinate pair.
coordinate_provenance_records.chronology_text: Historical and archaeological dromedary range context
coordinate_provenance_records.interpretation_note: Camel now keeps one named-place resolution trail instead of only a broad Arabian/Levant centroid.

site_evidence_records.site_label: Arabian Peninsula and Levant dromedary context
site_evidence_records.exact_source_text: The remains of a single large-sized Late Pleistocene camel individual recovered from the Site 1040 near Wadi Halfa were first evaluated by Gautier.
site_evidence_records.chronology_text: Historical and archaeological dromedary range context
site_evidence_records.interpretation_note: The paper names an exact Wadi Halfa specimen, but the current repository still publishes camel only as broad non-Nordic context rather than a precise atlas-grade point.
site_evidence_records.support_gap_note: The shipped point is still a broad contextual centroid even though the paper names Site 1040 near Wadi Halfa.

locality_records.nordic_inclusion_reason: The current curated project inventory does not yet identify a shipped Nordic-localized lead for this record.
locality_records.interpretation_note: No location evidence has been extracted yet for this recovered sample row.

project_summaries.paper_title: Combined hybridization capture and shotgun sequencing for ancient DNA analysis of extinct wild and domestic dromedary camel
project_summaries.nordic_relevance_reason: The current curated project inventory does not yet identify a shipped Nordic-localized lead for this record.
project_summaries.interpretation_caveat: Dromedary mitogenome range named on the primary paper page. It supports Camelus dromedarius only, not other camelids. This project stays in the evidence base for comparative context, not as shipped Nordic evidence.
project_summaries.notes: Dromedary mitogenome range named on the primary paper page. It supports Camelus dromedarius only, not other camelids.

project_summaries.paper_title: Combined hybridization capture and shotgun sequencing for ancient DNA analysis of extinct wild and domestic dromedary camel
project_summaries.nordic_relevance_reason: The current curated project inventory does not yet identify a shipped Nordic-localized lead for this record.
project_summaries.interpretation_caveat: Domestic-dromedary anchor. Comparator camelid evidence does not extend support to other camelid species automatically. This project stays in the evidence base for comparative context, not as shipped Nordic evidence.
project_summaries.notes: Domestic-dromedary anchor. Comparator camelid evidence does not extend support to other camelid species automatically.

study_summaries.paper_title: Combined hybridization capture and shotgun sequencing for ancient DNA analysis of extinct wild and domestic dromedary camel

normalization_scope: Non-human normalization currently governs accession-backed sample master rows, project summaries, study summaries, and curated locality summaries. Site and chronology extraction still vary by accession and remain explicit in sample inclusion status rather than being silently flattened.
